title: Potential End of War Tests Trump’s Promise of Quick Economic Rebound
summary: Gas prices and other goods could remain elevated for months, adding to the political challenge facing the White House in the midterm elections.

summary: “The struggle has not ended,” a defiant Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told Israelis.

title: Gov. Gavin Newsom Says Trump Is Investigating Him and His Wife
summary: Aides to Gov. Gavin Newsom of California say several people associated with the couple have been contacted by federal agents in the past week. He criticized the move as politically motivated.

title: Algae Is Turning the Reflecting Pool Green. Again.
summary: Algal blooms have hit the site, between the Lincoln Memorial and the Washington Monument, after a $14.2 million repair project.

summary: In a letter to Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and national parks officials, several Democrats and a Senate independent said that members of the administration could face fines and even criminal prosecution.

title: Kennedy Seeks to Expedite Appeal of Ruling That Blocked His Vaccine Policies

summary: The first half of the year had few big, buzzy hits. But there were some nice surprises (“Widow’s Bay”) and returning favorites (“The Comeback”).

title: How Kratom, an Addictive Gas Station Drug, Found Allies in Trump’s Cabinet
summary: With support from Markwayne Mullin and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the kratom industry is pursuing a potentially lucrative policy. Mr. Mullin owns equity in a company that could benefit.

title: The Pain of Caring for a Parent Who Abused You
summary: The United States is reliant on unpaid family caregivers, and millions of adult children are caring for parents who didn’t really care for them.

summary: A rift in Montana between the Democratic nominee and an independent candidate could boost Republican hopes of holding on to an open seat in conservative territory.

summary: Republicans had accused Democrats of attempting to confuse voters by boosting a candidate with the same name as the incumbent Republican senator.

title: 8 Dead in B-52 Bomber Crash at Edwards Air Force Base in California
summary: The aircraft burst into flames on Monday morning shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in California, officials said. The cause is still under investigation.
